title: eval-bench task
description: One JSON object per line in tasks.jsonl. Defines a single eval task for a recipe.

id.description: Stable task identifier; used as a key for fingerprinting and slicing.

description.description: Human-readable description. Must be unambiguous: two domain experts should independently agree on pass/fail without further discussion.

input.description: Inputs passed to the recipe under test. Shape is recipe-specific.

expected.description: Reference solution or expected properties. Optional for capability tasks; recommended for regression tasks.

axes.description: Slice axes for the Slice Explorer. Add any axis the recipe author cares about; common: model, complexity, language, framework, time_of_day.

polarity.description: positive = behaviour should fire; negative = behaviour should NOT fire. The harness rejects task sets that are 100% positive (one-sided eval anti-pattern).

failure_modes_targeted.description: IDs of failure-modes this task is designed to detect. Empty for synthetic tasks; populated for tasks derived from real failures.

tags.description: Suite membership. Must include at least one of `regression` or `capability`.

min_passk_target.description: Minimum acceptable pass^k for this task in the regression suite. Defaults to the recipe-level target.

source.description: Provenance: where this task came from. Useful for the closed-loop production-to-eval feedback.

ref.description: Stable reference: trace id, defect key, etc.
